acceptanceReviewed.criterion: A named remote output can be created on the Linux host.
Output can be parked without disrupting the developer session.
acceptanceReviewed.evidence: evidence/m1-remote-output-lifecycle/hyprland-monitors-after-park.json keeps DP-2/DP-3 and parks the named output at 50000x50000.
Adapter tests call remove twice; live evidence confirms final absence after remove.

acceptanceReviewed.criterion: Restart or stale-output reconciliation behavior is typed and tested.
acceptanceReviewed.evidence: adapter_lifecycle reconciliation test asserts typed ReconcileReport counters for missing desired and stale current outputs.

acceptanceReviewed.criterion: Hardware evidence includes command logs and Hyprland state before and after lifecycle operations.
acceptanceReviewed.evidence: commands.log, host-env.txt, and before/after Hyprland JSON are present under evidence/m1-remote-output-lifecycle.

realWorldValidation.evidence: Live Hyprland 0.55.2 lifecycle evidence collected on 2026-07-03.

resolvedFindings.title: Hyprland 0.55 keyword monitor no-op avoided
resolvedFindings.observed: hyprctl keyword monitor exits 0 on Hyprland 0.55.2 but reports that keyword cannot work with non-legacy parsers and did not change monitor state.
resolvedFindings.expected: The adapter must not report configuration success unless Hyprland state reflects the requested output configuration.
resolvedFindings.summary: The adapter now uses hyprctl eval with hl.monitor(...) and verifies monitor JSON after create/configure/park.
